title: Advanced participle clauses
explanation:
Advanced participle clauses

C1 users combine perfect, passive and negative participle clauses for concise formal style.

  • Having been warned repeatedly, he ignored the advice.
  • Seen from above, the city looks surprisingly green.
  • Not having been consulted, staff opposed the plan.

questions.text: [*Having been warned repeatedly|Warned repeatedly having|To warn repeatedly], he still ignored the advice.
[*Seen from above|Seeing from above|To see from above], the city looks surprisingly green.
[*Not having been consulted|Not consulting|Not to consult], staff opposed the plan.
[*Assuming the data are accurate|Assumed the data accurate|To assume accurate data], the trend is worrying.
[*Compared with last year|Comparing with last year|To compare last year], profits have risen.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: ____ by the evidence, the committee changed its recommendation. | *Convinced | Convincing | To convince
____ completed the survey, researchers analysed the responses. | *Having | Have | Had
____ into account inflation, wages have barely grown. | *Taken | Taking | Take
____ what she had said, he revised his conclusion. | *Remembering | Remembered | Remember
[*Given the limited budget|Giving the limited budget|To give limited budget], the project was delayed.
[*Having signed the contract|Signing the contract|Signed the contract], they began work immediately.
[*Viewed in context|Viewing in context|To view context], the remark seems less offensive.
[*Unless told otherwise|Unless telling otherwise|Unless to tell otherwise], assume the meeting is on.
[*Broadly speaking|Broad speaking|To speak broadly], the policy has succeeded.
